_meta.name: Afro-anarchist / afro-punk / afro-funk / afro-futurist / afro-art books — reference catalog
_meta.note: Curated set of real published books across every facet of afro-anarchist thought, music, science fiction, art and literature. Each entry carries multi-source buy-links — Bookshop.org, AK Press, PM Press, Verso, OpenLibrary (free reads where in PD), Internet Archive, Worldcat — built as deterministic search URLs so they keep working even when individual product pages move. The Library tab on the public site reads from this file. Verify ISBN before commercial reprints.

books.title: African Anarchism: The History of a Movement
books.author: Sam Mbah & I.E. Igariwey
books.summary: The reference text for African anarchist tradition — written from Lagos, traces acephalous societies through to contemporary movements.

books.title: Anarchism and the Black Revolution
books.author: Lorenzo Kom'boa Ervin
books.summary: Expanded edition of Ervin's foundational 1979 pamphlet — the most-circulated Black-anarchist text in English.

books.title: A Soldier's Story: Revolutionary Writings by a New Afrikan Anarchist
books.author: Kuwasi Balagoon
books.summary: Black Liberation Army veteran turned anarchist — collected essays, communiqués, and trial statements.

books.title: Pan-African Social Ecology
books.author: Modibo Kadalie
books.publisher: Daraja Press / On Our Own Authority!
books.summary: Speeches, conversations, and essays on autonomous mass democracy and African ecological traditions.

books.title: Black Anarchism: A Reader
books.author: Various / ed. Black Rose Anarchist Federation
books.summary: Anthology of Black-anarchist thought from Ervin, Alston, Balagoon and others.

books.title: Anarchy Works
books.author: Peter Gelderloos
books.summary: Cross-cultural survey of stateless societies — many drawn from African examples.

books.title: The Wretched of the Earth
books.author: Frantz Fanon
books.summary: Fanon's last work — colonialism, decolonial violence, and the psychology of liberation.

books.title: Black Skin, White Masks
books.author: Frantz Fanon
books.summary: Fanon's psychiatric and philosophical analysis of race in the colonial world.

books.title: How Europe Underdeveloped Africa
books.author: Walter Rodney
books.summary: Foundational structural argument: European capitalism actively underdeveloped Africa.

books.title: Discourse on Colonialism
books.author: Aimé Césaire
books.summary: Foundational anti-colonial pamphlet — the boomerang of colonial violence returning to Europe as fascism.

books.title: The Black Jacobins
books.author: C.L.R. James
books.summary: Toussaint L'Ouverture and the Haitian Revolution — the only successful slave revolt in modern history.

books.title: Decolonising the Mind
books.author: Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
books.summary: Why Ngũgĩ stopped writing in English — language as the politics of mind.

books.title: Necropolitics
books.author: Achille Mbembe
books.summary: Sovereignty as the power to dictate who lives and who dies.

books.title: On the Postcolony
books.author: Achille Mbembe
books.summary: Mbembe's anatomy of African post-independence states.

books.title: Black Marxism: The Making of the Black Radical Tradition
books.author: Cedric J. Robinson
books.summary: Robinson's case for racial capitalism — and a Black radical tradition older than Marxism.

books.title: Neo-Colonialism: The Last Stage of Imperialism
books.author: Kwame Nkrumah
books.summary: Nkrumah's case that political independence without economic sovereignty is no independence.

books.title: Parable of the Sower
books.author: Octavia E. Butler
books.summary: Near-future Los Angeles collapse, religion of Earthseed — Butler's prophetic afrofuturist masterpiece.

books.title: Kindred
books.author: Octavia E. Butler
books.summary: A Black woman writer pulled back to antebellum Maryland — time-travel as moral confrontation.

books.title: Wild Seed
books.author: Octavia E. Butler
books.summary: Two immortals across centuries of African and American history — the founding text of the Patternist series.

books.title: The Fifth Season
books.author: N.K. Jemisin
books.summary: Hugo-winning first volume of the Broken Earth trilogy — geological violence and structural oppression.

books.title: How Long 'Til Black Future Month?
books.author: N.K. Jemisin
books.summary: Jemisin's short-fiction collection — the title essay names a movement.

books.title: Binti
books.author: Nnedi Okorafor
books.summary: Hugo & Nebula-winning novella — a Himba woman travels to a galactic university through a war.

books.title: Who Fears Death
books.author: Nnedi Okorafor
books.summary: Post-apocalyptic Saharan Africa — magic, weaponized rape, and revenge.

books.title: Riot Baby
books.author: Tochi Onyebuchi
books.summary: A sister with telekinesis, a brother in prison — Black rage as supernatural force.

books.title: Sheree Renée Thomas — Dark Matter (anthology)
books.author: ed. Sheree Renée Thomas
books.summary: Foundational anthology of Black speculative fiction — Du Bois, Delany, Hopkinson, Butler, Bradley.

books.title: Fela: The Life and Times of an African Musical Icon
books.author: Michael Veal
books.summary: Definitive English-language biography of Fela Kuti — Afrobeat as political weapon.

books.title: Fela: This Bitch of a Life
books.author: Carlos Moore
books.summary: First-person Fela biography — recorded by Carlos Moore in Lagos.

books.title: Tony Allen: An Autobiography of the Master Drummer of Afrobeat
books.author: Tony Allen with Michael Veal
books.summary: The drummer who co-invented Afrobeat with Fela tells his story.

books.title: Thelonious Monk: The Life and Times of an American Original
books.author: Robin D.G. Kelley
books.summary: Definitive Monk biography by the historian who later wrote Africa Speaks, America Answers.

books.title: Africa Speaks, America Answers: Modern Jazz in Revolutionary Times
books.author: Robin D.G. Kelley
books.summary: How modern jazz and African independence movements remade each other.

books.title: Funk: The Music, the People, and the Rhythm of the One
books.author: Rickey Vincent
books.summary: Cultural history of funk from James Brown forward, with deep political analysis.

books.title: Space Is the Place: The Lives and Times of Sun Ra
books.author: John F. Szwed
books.summary: Sun Ra biography — Saturn, Egyptian cosmology, the Arkestra, the original afrofuturist.

books.title: Afro-Punk: The 'Rock N Roll Nigger' Experience (companion)
books.author: James Spooner
books.summary: Companion to Spooner's documentary that named the AfroPunk movement; his 2022 graphic-memoir 'The High Desert' continues the story.

books.title: The High Desert: Black. Punk. Nowhere.
books.author: James Spooner
books.summary: Spooner's graphic memoir of a Black punk teenager in 1980s California.

books.title: Right to Rock: The Black Rock Coalition and the Cultural Politics of Race
books.author: Maureen Mahon
books.summary: Ethnography of the Black Rock Coalition — Living Colour, 24-7 Spyz, Fishbone.

books.title: Bad Brains: A Band in DC
books.author: Lustmord & Bad Brains
books.summary: Photobook + history of hardcore's foundational Black band.

books.title: Sister Outsider
books.author: Audre Lorde
books.summary: Fifteen essays and speeches from Lorde — Black, lesbian, mother, warrior, poet.

books.title: Women, Race & Class
books.author: Angela Y. Davis
books.summary: Davis's foundational synthesis of feminism and Black liberation.

books.title: Ain't I a Woman: Black Women and Feminism
books.author: bell hooks
books.summary: hooks's first book — the foundational text on Black women in U.S. feminism.

books.title: Feminism Is for Everybody
books.author: bell hooks
books.summary: hooks's accessible introduction to feminist thought.

books.title: Black Feminist Thought
books.author: Patricia Hill Collins
books.summary: Foundational sociological framework for Black feminist epistemology.

books.title: Are Prisons Obsolete?
books.author: Angela Y. Davis
books.summary: Davis's introductory case for prison abolition — short, urgent, foundational.

books.title: Golden Gulag
books.author: Ruth Wilson Gilmore
books.summary: Geography of the California prison-building boom — the carceral counter-revolution.

books.title: Abolition Geography
books.author: Ruth Wilson Gilmore
books.summary: Twelve essays from the leading theorist of carceral geography.

books.title: No More Police: A Case for Abolition
books.author: Mariame Kaba & Andrea J. Ritchie
books.summary: A practical, programmatic abolitionist text from two of the movement's leading organisers.

books.title: Things Fall Apart
books.author: Chinua Achebe
books.summary: Foundational text of African literature — the colonial encounter in Igboland.

books.title: Beloved
books.author: Toni Morrison
books.summary: Pulitzer-winning novel — the ghost of slavery refuses to be exorcised.

books.title: Half of a Yellow Sun
books.author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
books.summary: The Biafran war as told through three lives.

books.title: Between the World and Me
books.author: Ta-Nehisi Coates
books.summary: Letter from a Black father to his son on living in a Black body in America.

books.title: The Fire Next Time
books.author: James Baldwin
books.summary: Baldwin's two essays on race in America — still incandescent.

books.title: The Color Purple
books.author: Alice Walker
books.summary: Pulitzer-winning epistolary novel of survival and Black womanist love.

books.title: Lagoon
books.author: Nnedi Okorafor
books.summary: First contact off the coast of Lagos — Okorafor's ecological sci-fi.

books.title: Everfair
books.author: Nisi Shawl
books.summary: Steampunk alternate history of the Belgian Congo — a Black-led utopia challenges Leopold's reign.

books.title: Shadowshaper
books.author: Daniel José Older
books.summary: Brooklyn-set urban-fantasy — Afro-Latine spirits painted into murals.

books.title: Out of the Dark Night: Essays on Decolonization
books.author: Achille Mbembe
books.summary: Mbembe on the planetary stakes of decolonization.

books.title: Staying Alive: Women, Ecology, and Development
books.author: Vandana Shiva
books.summary: Shiva's eco-feminist critique of development — read alongside Kadalie's Pan-African Social Ecology.

books.title: The Ecology of Freedom
books.author: Murray Bookchin
books.summary: Foundational social ecology text — Kadalie's Pan-African Social Ecology builds on it.
